name: Iusaaset
description: Iusaaset is an ancient Egyptian primordial goddess, often associated with Atum, the creator god. She is considered a grandmother of the gods and a protector of pharaohs. Iusaaset represents the feminine principle complementing Atum's masculine aspect, embodying the concept of completion and fulfillment. She is a relatively obscure deity, but her importance lies in her connection to the creation myth and the royal ideology. Iusaaset's name translates to 'Great One who comes forth, she is great', reflecting her role as a source of creation and power. She is linked to the acacia tree, a symbol of rebirth and immortality, and is sometimes depicted with a headdress featuring the acacia fruit. While not widely worshipped in temples like other major deities, her presence is felt through her influence on the creation narrative and her association with the royal family. Iusaaset ensures the continuity of the royal line and the stability of the cosmos, solidifying her position as a vital, though often overlooked, figure in Egyptian mythology. Her powers are tied to creation, protection, and the ensurement of cosmic order. She is a divine grandmother, ensuring the well-being of the divine and royal families. She is a primordial force, existing alongside Atum from the beginning, contributing to the completeness of creation. Iusaaset's role emphasizes the importance of the feminine divine in the Egyptian pantheon, showcasing the balance between male and female energies in the creation and maintenance of the world.
subtitle: Primordial Grandmother, Protector of Pharaohs

keyMyths.title: Iusaaset and the Creation
keyMyths.description: Iusaaset is believed to have emerged alongside Atum at the dawn of creation, representing the feminine counterpart necessary for complete creation. She embodies the potential for life and growth.

keyMyths.title: Protection of the Pharaohs
keyMyths.description: Iusaaset is considered a protector of the pharaohs, ensuring the continuation of the royal line and the stability of the kingdom. She is invoked in rituals to safeguard the pharaoh's well-being.

keyMyths.title: The Acacia Tree
keyMyths.description: The acacia tree is sacred to Iusaaset, symbolizing rebirth and immortality. This association links her to themes of regeneration and the cyclical nature of life.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.description: Detailed profiles of numerous Egyptian gods and goddesses.
sources.text: Detailed profiles of numerous Egyptian gods and goddesses.
